# Plan: `coc-transform` ŌĆö AI-Generated Process Titles

## Overview

Four atomic commits to thread a human-readable `title` from the AI SDK layer all the way to the SPA dashboard, using `gpt-4.1` as the default model for title generation.

| # | Commit Scope | Key File(s) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Schema Update | `packages/pipeline-core/src/ai/process-types.ts` |
| 2 | SDK Implementation | `packages/pipeline-core/src/copilot-sdk-wrapper/copilot-sdk-service.ts` |
| 3 | Bridge Integration | `packages/coc/src/server/queue-executor-bridge.ts` |
| 4 | UI Exposure | `packages/coc/src/server/queue-handler.ts` |

---

## Commit 1 ŌĆö Schema Update: Add `title` to `AIProcess`

### Goal
Add an optional `title` field to `AIProcess` so every layer can read and write it without breaking existing consumers.

### Files
- `packages/pipeline-core/src/ai/process-types.ts`

### Implementation Steps

1. Locate the `AIProcess` interface (line ~332).
2. Add the new field inside the **Conversation Fields** block (or just before it) to keep related context fields grouped:

```ts
/** Human-readable title generated by AI after the first exchange */
title?: string;
```

3. No migration is needed ŌĆö the field is optional and the `FileProcessStore` serialises `AIProcess` as JSON, so old records without `title` will deserialise cleanly with `title === undefined`.

### Test Strategy
- No new unit test required for the interface change itself.
- Verify that existing `pipeline-core` Vitest suite still passes (`npm run test:run` inside `packages/pipeline-core`).
- Verify the extension Mocha suite still passes (`npm run test` from root).

### Verification Criteria
- `AIProcess` has `title?: string` in the type definition.
- TypeScript compilation succeeds (`npm run build`).
- No regressions in existing tests.

---

## Commit 2 ŌĆö SDK Implementation: Add `transform<T>` to `CopilotSDKService`

### Goal
Provide a reusable, lightweight method that sends a short one-shot prompt to the AI and returns a typed result. This keeps title-generation logic out of the bridge and makes it independently testable.

### Files
- `packages/pipeline-core/src/copilot-sdk-wrapper/copilot-sdk-service.ts`
- `packages/pipeline-core/src/copilot-sdk-wrapper/types.ts` (if a new `TransformOptions` type is needed)
- `packages/pipeline-core/src/index.ts` (export the new method signature if not already via the class)

### Signature

```ts
/**
 * Sends a one-shot prompt and returns a parsed value of type T.
 * Uses `gpt-4.1` by default. Throws on AI unavailability or parse failure.
 *
 * @param prompt  The instruction sent to the model.
 * @param parse   Map the raw AI response string to T. Defaults to identity cast.
 * @param options Override model, timeout, or working directory.
 */
public async transform<T = string>(
    prompt: string,
    parse?: (raw: string) => T,
    options?: { model?: string; timeoutMs?: number; cwd?: string }
): Promise<T>
```

### Implementation Steps

1. Add `transform<T>` as a public method on `CopilotSDKService` (near the bottom of the class, after `sendFollowUp`).
2. Internally call `this.sendMessage(...)` with:
   - `model: options?.model ?? 'gpt-4.1'`
   - `timeout: options?.timeoutMs ?? DEFAULT_AI_TIMEOUT_MS`
   - `keepAlive: false` (stateless call)
   - `workingDirectory: options?.cwd`
3. Extract `result.content` (the raw text response).
4. Apply `parse(raw)` if provided, otherwise return `raw as unknown as T`.
5. Wrap the call in a try/catch; re-throw with a descriptive message on failure.

### Default Model
`gpt-4.1` is used as the default because it is fast and cheap for short classification/summarisation tasks. Callers can override via `options.model`.

### Test Strategy
- Add a unit test file: `packages/pipeline-core/src/copilot-sdk-wrapper/__tests__/transform.test.ts`
- Mock `sendMessage` to return a fixed `SDKInvocationResult`.
- Test cases:
  - Returns raw string when no `parse` function is provided.
  - Applies `parse` function and returns typed result.
  - Uses `gpt-4.1` by default (assert the model passed to `sendMessage`).
  - Propagates error from `sendMessage` as a descriptive `Error`.

### Verification Criteria
- `transform<T>` is exported from `pipeline-core` public API.
- Unit tests pass.
- `npm run build` succeeds.

---

## Commit 3 ŌĆö Bridge Integration: Call `transform` in `QueueExecutorBridge`

### Goal
After a chat/clarification task produces its first AI response, call `transform` to generate a concise title and persist it on the `AIProcess` record.

### Files
- `packages/coc/src/server/queue-executor-bridge.ts`

### When to Generate the Title
Generate the title **once**, after the first AI response turn is appended to `conversationTurns` and before the process is marked `completed`. Re-generating on every turn is wasteful; check `!process.title` to skip if already set.

### Prompt Template

```
Summarise the following user message as a short title (max 8 words, no punctuation):

"{{FIRST_USER_MESSAGE}}"
```

Replace `{{FIRST_USER_MESSAGE}}` with the first `role === 'user'` turn's `content`, truncated to 400 characters.

### Implementation Steps

1. Import `transform` (or call it via the `CopilotSDKService` instance already available in the bridge).
2. Locate the section where the bridge calls `store.updateProcess(processId, { conversationTurns: [...] })`.
3. After writing the first user turn:

```ts
if (!existingProcess.title) {
    const firstUserContent = turns.find(t => t.role === 'user')?.content ?? '';
    const truncated = firstUserContent.substring(0, 400);
    try {
        const title = await sdkService.transform<string>(
            `Summarise the following user message as a short title (max 8 words, no punctuation):\n\n"${truncated}"`,
            raw => raw.trim().replace(/[".]/g, ''),
            { model: 'gpt-4.1', cwd: workingDirectory }
        );
        await store.updateProcess(processId, { title });
    } catch (err) {
        logger.warn(LogCategory.Queue, `Title generation failed for ${processId}`, err);
        // Non-fatal: proceed without title
    }
}
```

4. Ensure the title generation is **fire-and-continue** (do not block the main response stream on it). Use `void titlePromise` or run after `await store.updateProcess(...)` for the conversation update.

### Test Strategy
- Add or extend `packages/coc/src/__tests__/queue-executor-bridge.test.ts`.
- Mock `CopilotSDKService.transform` to return `'Mock Title'`.
- Assert that `store.updateProcess` is called with `{ title: 'Mock Title' }` after the first user turn.
- Assert it is **not** called again on subsequent turns (idempotency).
- Assert title generation failure does not throw or abort the task.

### Verification Criteria
- `AIProcess.title` is populated in the process store after the first user turn of a `chat` task.
- Failure of title generation is logged as a warning, not an error; the task continues.
- Existing bridge tests still pass.

---

## Commit 4 ŌĆö UI Exposure: Display `title` in `enrichChatTasks`

### Goal
Surface `AIProcess.title` in the API response so the SPA dashboard can display a meaningful name instead of a raw `processId` or truncated prompt.

### Files
- `packages/coc/src/server/queue-handler.ts`

### Implementation Steps

1. Locate `enrichChatTasks` (line ~269).
2. Inside the loop, after `process` is fetched, read `process.title`:

```ts
task.chatMeta = {
    turnCount: turns.length,
    firstMessage: firstUserTurn ? ... : undefined,
    lastActivityAt,
    title: process.title,   // ŌåÉ add this line
};
```

3. The `title` field will be `undefined` for older processes (no schema migration needed).
4. Update the `chatMeta` shape documentation/JSDoc if one exists.

### Test Strategy
- Add a test case in `packages/coc/src/__tests__/queue-handler.test.ts` (or the equivalent spec file).
- Provide a mock `ProcessStore.getProcess` that returns an `AIProcess` with `title: 'Test Title'`.
- Assert that the resulting `task.chatMeta.title` equals `'Test Title'`.
- Add a case where `process.title` is `undefined` to confirm `chatMeta.title` is `undefined` (no crash).

### Verification Criteria
- `GET /api/tasks` (or equivalent) response includes `chatMeta.title` when a title has been generated.
- `chatMeta.title` is absent/`undefined` for processes without a title ŌĆö no `null` pollution.
- All existing `queue-handler` tests pass.

---

## Cross-Cutting Concerns

### Default Model
All title-generation calls use `gpt-4.1` by default. This is:
- Configured as the default in `CopilotSDKService.transform`.
- Overridable per-call via `options.model`.
- Not stored in any global config, so it can be changed per-callsite without breaking other flows.

### Error Handling
Title generation is **best-effort**:
- A failure in Commit 2 (`transform`) throws ŌĆö callers decide whether to swallow it.
- Commit 3 (bridge) catches and logs a warning; the task proceeds normally.
- Commit 4 (UI) reads an optional field; no special handling required.

### Backward Compatibility
- `AIProcess.title` is optional ŌĆö existing serialised records without it deserialise fine.
- `chatMeta.title` is optional in the API response ŌĆö existing SPA code that doesn't read it is unaffected.

### Running Tests
```bash
# From repo root
npm run build          # Compile all packages

# pipeline-core unit tests
cd packages/pipeline-core && npm run test:run

# coc unit tests
cd packages/coc && npm run test:run

# Full extension test suite
cd ../.. && npm run test
```
